name: TOEFL 2026 Speaking — Listen and Repeat

criteria.name: Repetition Exactness
criteria.description: How completely and exactly the utterance reproduces the prompt. Level 5 = an exact repetition; level 4 = captures the meaning with minor word/grammar changes; level 3 = contains a majority of content words but changes meaning; level 2 = a significant part is missing; level 1 = very little of the prompt.

criteria.name: Meaning Preservation
criteria.description: Whether the original meaning survives, accounting for missing/changed function words, tense/number markers, transpositions, and related-word substitutions.

criteria.name: Intelligibility
criteria.description: How understandable the speech is to a listener unfamiliar with the prompt; occasional to pervasive intelligibility problems lower the score.

instructions: Official 2026 ETS 'Listen and Repeat' task, scored 0-5 per sentence. The test-taker hears one short sentence once and repeats it exactly. In this app the primary signal is automatic word-overlap accuracy between the target and the ASR transcript; this rubric documents the official facets and can drive optional LLM commentary. The 0-5 task scores feed the 1.0-6.0 Speaking section band.
